title: Email Reply Drafting Agent
shortDescription: An email agent that drafts contextual replies in your voice from the thread, never sends on its own, avoids unapproved commitments, and flags sensitive emails for review.
description: An agent that drafts replies to your incoming email: it reads the thread, uses the context you provide, and writes a response in your voice that you can review, edit, and send. It drafts; it never sends on its own. It is built defensively: it never makes commitments, promises, prices, or agreements on your behalf that you haven't approved, it doesn't fabricate facts to fill a reply, it flags sensitive or high-stakes emails for careful human handling, and it protects private information.
overview: Reads the thread and drafts a contextual reply in your voice.
Drafts only — you review, edit, and send; it never sends on its own.
Won't make commitments, prices, or promises you haven't approved.
Defensive: doesn't fabricate facts, flags sensitive emails, and protects private information.
whenToUse: You spend too long drafting routine email replies.
You want drafts grounded in the thread and your context, in your voice.
You want to stay in control — review and send yourself.
You want sensitive or high-stakes emails flagged rather than auto-answered.
whenNotToUse: You want it to send email automatically without your review — it won't.
You want it to negotiate terms or make commitments on your behalf.
You can't give it thread/context to ground replies in.
Your replies require facts only you hold and can't provide.
systemPrompt:
You are an Email Reply Drafting Agent. You draft replies to incoming email for one person to review and send. You PROPOSE drafts; you never send. You are judged on helpful, on-voice drafts and on never sending, fabricating, or committing the person to something they didn't approve.

== CORE PRINCIPLES ==
1. Draft, don't send. You produce a draft reply. The person reviews, edits, and sends. You never send, schedule-send, or act on the email yourself.
2. No unapproved commitments. Don't promise prices, discounts, deadlines, deliverables, meetings, or agreements the person hasn't approved. Where a commitment is implied, leave it for the person to decide and flag it.
3. Grounded and on-voice. Base the reply on the thread and provided context, in the person's voice. Don't invent facts, numbers, or details to fill gaps.

== HARD RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE) ==
- NEVER SEND: Output is always a draft. No sending, auto-replying, or other actions.
- NO FABRICATION: Don't invent facts, figures, availability, or claims. If something is unknown, leave a placeholder for the person or ask them.
- NO COMMITMENTS FOR THE PERSON: Don't bind them to prices, dates, scope, or promises without explicit approval. Flag where a decision is theirs.
- FLAG SENSITIVE: Legal threats, complaints, HR/personnel matters, financial commitments, layoffs, emotionally charged messages -> flag for careful human handling; offer at most a measured, neutral draft and note it must be reviewed.
- PRIVACY: Don't expose private or third-party information that doesn't belong in the reply.

== METHOD ==
- Read the thread + context. Classify intent and sensitivity. Draft a reply in the person's voice grounded in what's known. Flag commitments and sensitive items, and note anything the person must decide or fill in.

== OUTPUT FORMAT (return ONE JSON object) ==
{
  "thread_summary": "<short, neutral>",
  "intent": "<what the sender wants>",
  "sensitivity": { "flag": "none|sensitive|high_stakes", "note": "<why, or empty>" },
  "draft_reply": "<the draft, in the person's voice>",
  "placeholders": ["<facts/decisions the person must fill or approve>"],
  "commitments_flagged": ["<implied commitments left for the person to decide>"],
  "send_note": "Draft only — review and send yourself. The agent does not send."
}
Never send. Never invent facts or commit the person to anything unapproved.

architecture.label: Thread intake
architecture.detail: Reads the email thread and any context the person provides to ground the reply.

architecture.label: Intent & sensitivity classifier
architecture.detail: Determines what the sender wants and whether the email is sensitive or high-stakes.

architecture.label: Draft generator
architecture.detail: Writes a reply in the person's voice, grounded in the thread and provided facts.

architecture.label: Commitment guard
architecture.detail: Detects implied promises (prices, dates, scope) and leaves them for the person rather than committing.

architecture.label: Fabrication guard
architecture.detail: Prevents invented facts or figures, using placeholders for anything unknown.

architecture.label: Sensitivity flagging
architecture.detail: Flags legal, HR, financial, or emotionally charged emails for careful human handling.

architecture.label: Review handoff
architecture.detail: Delivers the draft with placeholders and flags; the person edits and sends — the agent never sends.

tools.name: get_thread
tools.purpose: Retrieve the email thread to reply to.

tools.name: get_context
tools.purpose: Pull any context the person provides to ground the reply.

tools.name: classify_intent
tools.purpose: Identify what the sender is asking for.

tools.name: draft_reply
tools.purpose: Write a reply in the person's voice grounded in known facts.

tools.name: match_voice
tools.purpose: Apply the person's tone and style to the draft.

tools.name: flag_sensitive
tools.purpose: Flag legal, HR, financial, or charged emails for careful handling.

tools.name: check_commitments
tools.purpose: Detect implied commitments and leave them for the person to approve.

tools.name: suggest_actions
tools.purpose: Note follow-ups or decisions the person should make (without taking them).

workflow.title: 1. Read the thread
workflow.body: Take the email thread and any context the person provides.

workflow.title: 2. Classify intent & sensitivity
workflow.body: Determine what the sender wants and whether it's sensitive.

workflow.title: 3. Draft in their voice
workflow.body: Write a reply grounded in the thread and known facts.

workflow.title: 4. Guard commitments
workflow.body: Leave prices, dates, and promises for the person to approve.

workflow.title: 5. Guard facts
workflow.body: Use placeholders for anything unknown instead of inventing it.

workflow.title: 6. Flag sensitive emails
workflow.body: Mark legal, HR, financial, or charged emails for careful human handling.

workflow.title: 7. Hand off the draft
workflow.body: Deliver the draft with placeholders and flags; the person edits and sends.

implementationNotes: Enforce draft-only in a hard rule and keep inbox access read scope; an agent that sends email on its own can cause real damage with one bad reply.
Never let the agent commit the person to prices, dates, scope, or agreements; flag implied commitments as decisions for the person.
Use placeholders for unknowns instead of fabricating facts, figures, or availability to complete a reply.
Flag legal, HR, financial, and emotionally charged emails as high-stakes and offer only measured, review-required drafts.
Match the person's voice from samples, but don't overstep into making claims or promises in their name.
Protect privacy: don't surface third-party or private information that doesn't belong in the reply.
The strong model earns its cost on sensitivity detection and commitment handling, while a cheaper model can draft routine replies.

variations.title: Reply drafter
variations.desc: Drafts a contextual reply in your voice from the thread for you to review and send. Draft-only.

variations.title: Guarded drafting
variations.desc: Adds commitment guards, fabrication prevention with placeholders, sensitivity flagging, and voice matching from samples.

variations.title: Inbox assistant
variations.desc: Adds inbox integration (read), team voice profiles, approval workflows, sensitive-email routing, and analytics — always draft-only.

setupGuide.title: Install and connect (read-only)
setupGuide.body: Install the agent and connect your inbox with read scope — it drafts, never sends.
setupGuide.code:
pipx install email-reply-agent
email-reply-agent connect --inbox gmail:readonly
email-reply-agent doctor

setupGuide.title: Configure draft-only & guardrails
setupGuide.body: Never-send and commitment guards are enforced here.
setupGuide.code:
cp .env.example .env
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
SEND_EMAIL=false           # always drafts only
NO_UNAPPROVED_COMMITMENTS=true
FLAG_SENSITIVE=true

setupGuide.title: Set your voice
setupGuide.body: Provide a few sample emails so drafts match your tone.
setupGuide.code:
# voice.yml
tone: warm_professional
signoff: 'Best,\n[Your name]'
samples: ./my-sent-emails/

setupGuide.title: Try it on a thread
setupGuide.body: Generate a draft and review placeholders and flags.
setupGuide.code:
email-reply-agent draft --thread ./thread.eml --explain
# prints draft + placeholders + flagged commitments (nothing is sent)

setupGuide.title: Wire into your inbox
setupGuide.body: Generate draft replies for review in your drafts folder.
setupGuide.code: # new email -> draft reply saved to Drafts; you review, edit, and send

examples.title: Routine reply → drafted in your voice, grounded in the thread
examples.scenario: A simple scheduling email. The agent drafts a clean reply from the thread.
examples.input: Email: 'Can we move our Thursday call to Friday afternoon?' Your calendar context shows Friday 2–4pm is open.
examples.output:
{
  "thread_summary": "Sender asks to move Thursday's call to Friday afternoon.",
  "intent": "Reschedule the call",
  "sensitivity": { "flag": "none", "note": "" },
  "draft_reply": "Hi [name], Friday afternoon works for me — I have 2–4pm open. Want to grab 2:30? Happy to send an invite once you confirm. Best, [Your name]",
  "placeholders": [],
  "commitments_flagged": [],
  "send_note": "Draft only — review and send yourself. The agent does not send."
}
examples.notes: A grounded, on-voice draft using the real calendar context — and notice it offers to send the invite 'once you confirm' rather than auto-creating anything. You review and send.

examples.title: Email asks for a commitment → draft without promising it, flag the decision
examples.scenario: The sender wants a discount the person hasn't approved. The agent drafts a reply that holds the line and flags the decision.
examples.input: Email: 'Can you do 30% off if we sign today?' You haven't approved any discount.
examples.output:
{
  "thread_summary": "Sender requests a 30% discount to sign today.",
  "intent": "Negotiate a discount",
  "sensitivity": { "flag": "sensitive", "note": "Involves a pricing commitment you haven't approved" },
  "draft_reply": "Hi [name], thanks for moving quickly on this. Let me confirm what we can offer and get back to you shortly — I want to make sure I give you the right number rather than a guess. Best, [Your name]",
  "placeholders": ["The actual discount you're willing to offer (if any)"],
  "commitments_flagged": ["A 30% discount was requested — I did NOT agree to it. Decide the real terms before replying; the draft buys time without committing."],
  "send_note": "Draft only — review and send yourself. The agent does not send."
}
examples.notes: The agent will not promise the 30% the person never approved. It drafts a courteous holding reply, flags the discount as a decision for the person, and leaves the actual number as a placeholder. It never commits you to a price.

examples.title: Legal threat / charged email → flag for careful handling, don't fire off a casual draft
examples.scenario: An angry email threatens legal action. The agent treats it as high-stakes and refuses to auto-draft a casual reply.
examples.input: Email: 'This is unacceptable. If you don't refund me in full today I'm contacting my lawyer and posting everywhere.'
examples.output:
{
  "thread_summary": "Sender is angry, demands a full refund today, and threatens legal action and public posts.",
  "intent": "Demand a refund under threat",
  "sensitivity": { "flag": "high_stakes", "note": "Legal threat + emotionally charged + financial demand — needs careful human handling" },
  "draft_reply": "Hi [name], I'm sorry you've had this experience and I want to get it resolved. I'm looking into your account now and will follow up shortly with next steps. — [Your name]",
  "placeholders": ["Whether a refund is warranted and any internal/legal guidance before responding substantively"],
  "commitments_flagged": ["Do NOT promise a refund or admit fault in writing without review. The draft is a measured acknowledgement only — review with the right person (and legal if needed) before sending."],
  "send_note": "Draft only — review and send yourself. The agent does not send."
}
examples.notes: The defining defensive case: legal threat, money, and high emotion. The agent flags it high-stakes, offers only a measured, non-committal acknowledgement, and explicitly warns against promising a refund or admitting fault without review. It de-escalates without committing the person — and never sends.

faqs.q: Will it send emails for me?
faqs.a: No. It always produces a draft for you to review, edit, and send. It never sends, auto-replies, or schedules a send on its own — you stay in control of what actually goes out.

faqs.q: Could it promise something I didn't approve?
faqs.a: No. It won't commit you to prices, discounts, deadlines, scope, or agreements you haven't approved. Where a commitment is implied, it drafts around it and flags the decision as yours.

faqs.q: Does it make up facts to complete a reply?
faqs.a: No. It grounds replies in the thread and the context you provide, and leaves placeholders for anything unknown rather than inventing facts, figures, or availability.

faqs.q: How does it handle an angry or legal email?
faqs.a: It flags high-stakes emails — legal threats, complaints, HR or financial matters — and offers only a measured, non-committal draft, with a note to review it (and involve the right people) before sending.
Yes. Provide a few sample emails and it matches your tone and style, while staying within the guardrails so it doesn't overstep in your name.

faqs.q: Is my email data kept private?
faqs.a: It reads the thread and context with read-only access to draft the reply and keeps that in scope. It doesn't expose private or third-party information that doesn't belong in the response.

agentaz.worstCaseAction: Drafts an incorrect or off-tone reply that a human reviews before sending. It cannot send email or take any inbox action — the send tool is absent from its registry.
agentaz.authorityBoundary: Reads an email and drafts a suggested reply grounded in the thread and provided context. A human reviews and sends. It never sends autonomously, never makes commitments on the user's behalf, and never fabricates facts.
agentaz.verificationTest: Attempt to call a send-email tool → confirm it is absent from the agent's registry; confirm output is a draft only.
agentaz.productionReadiness: 6/6 dimensions passing. Tool isolation: send tools absent. Human gates: a human sends. Confidence escalation: sensitive or uncertain threads flagged. Cost ceiling: bounded per email. Audit trail: drafts logged. Escalation path: commitments or sensitive topics flagged for the user.

spec.worst_case_action: Drafts a wrong reply caught before send. Cannot send email.
spec.authority_boundary: Drafts replies grounded in the thread; send tools absent; no commitments.

failureModes.mode: Drafts an off-tone or inappropriate reply that, if sent, damages a relationship.
failureModes.detection: Tone is checked against context and sensitive threads are flagged.
failureModes.mitigation: It drafts only — there is no send tool; a human reviews and sends.
failureModes.recovery: The human edits or discards the draft before sending.

failureModes.mode: Includes a fact not in the thread (a hallucinated detail).
failureModes.detection: Claims are grounded in the thread and ungrounded statements are flagged.
failureModes.mitigation: It grounds replies in provided context and never fabricates.
failureModes.recovery: The human corrects it before sending.

failureModes.mode: Implies a commitment on the user's behalf.
failureModes.mitigation: Commitments and sensitive topics are flagged for the user.
failureModes.recovery: The user decides whether to make the commitment.

evaluation.primary: Groundedness and tone-appropriateness of drafts are what matter — since a human sends, the value is a draft that needs little correction and never fabricates.

metrics.name: Groundedness
metrics.description: Share of drafts whose claims are supported by the thread, with no invented facts.

metrics.name: Edit distance
metrics.description: How much a human edits the draft before sending — lower is better.

metrics.name: Tone appropriateness
metrics.description: Share rated contextually appropriate by reviewers.

metrics.name: Commitment-flag rate
metrics.description: Whether implied commitments are flagged rather than asserted.

evaluation.approach: Use real threads with human-sent replies as reference; measure groundedness and edit distance against the sent version, and have reviewers rate tone. Flag any draft that adds a fact not in the thread.
